experiences.intentions: Active piece is Z near the top, currently spanning columns around 4-6 with plenty of empty space below. Early game goal is to place pieces flat and avoid creating holes; with next pieces O,S,L,I, a horizontal Z can be dropped safely to the side, but first moving right helps place it toward the wall and preserve the center. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would lock it near center-top; rotate could create a vertical notch unnecessarily; left/right improve future placement. I choose right to shift the Z toward the right side before dropping.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L near the top center with open space below but an uneven stack in the middle. Immediate goal is to place it efficiently without creating overhangs; a good sub-goal is to orient the L vertically so it can descend into the central channel more cleanly. Candidate moves: left/right just shift the current horizontal L and don't improve fit yet; soft/hard drop now would commit to a weak placement high on the board; no_op wastes a turn. Rotating right to orientation 1 makes the piece vertical with foot to the right, which looks best for threading downward next turn.
